columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier; primary key and first component of the composite merge key used by sp_mysqltobq_load.sql for incremental upserts

columns.name: email
columns.description: Corporate email address; candidate for post-load uniqueness validation in data quality checks

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Contact phone number; nullable in source

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Date on which the employee was hired

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role code; used as third clustering column to accelerate role-based analytical queries

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Base salary with two decimal places of precision

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission rate expressed as a decimal percentage; null for non-commission roles

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referential foreign key to employee_id identifying the employee's direct manager; null for top-level managers

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier; used as first clustering column to support department-level aggregations and filters

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status code (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE); low-cardinality column used as second clustering column

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: UTC timestamp of original record creation in the MySQL source system

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent update in MySQL; partition column (DAY granularity) with 60-day expiry; second component of the composite merge key for incremental loads

cardinality.notes: Self-referential hierarchy: many employees report to one manager via manager_id -> employee_id; root managers carry a null manager_id. Rows where updated_date IS NULL will land in BigQuery's __NULL__ partition and are exempt from the 60-day partition expiry; this edge-case should be handled in the new-column handling and retention documentation.
